scenario: Free and paid features are clearly labelled
steps: Navigate to the main features area.
expected_result: Free and paid features are visually distinguishable before interaction.

scenario: Paid feature shows payment status before use
steps: Observe the screen before entering the feature flow.
expected_result: The feature is marked as paid before the user spends time entering the flow.

scenario: Free user can log a mood entry
steps: Launch the app using a non-subscribed account.
expected_result: The user can successfully record a mood entry without subscribing.

scenario: Free user can access basic mood history
steps: Launch the app using a non-subscribed account with previous check-ins.
expected_result: The user can view basic mood history without paying.

scenario: Insight summary appears after enough data been collected
steps: Log multiple mood entries over the required time period.
Navigate to the insights or summary section.
expected_result: A mood summary with identifiable trends is displayed.

scenario: Summary includes pattern information
steps: Use an account with sufficient mood history.
expected_result: The summary shows trends or patterns derived from the user's mood data.

scenario: Reflection prompts are delivered regularly
steps: Use the app over multiple scheduled periods.
Wait for or trigger reflection prompts.
Check whether prompts appear at expected times.
expected_result: The app provides structured reflection prompts on a recurring basis.

scenario: User can save and review journal reflections
steps: Open a reflection or journaling prompt.
Save the entry and reopen the history.
expected_result: The saved reflection appears in the user's history.

scenario: App opens without crashing
steps: Install or launch the app on a supported device.
Open the app from the home screen.
expected_result: The app launches successfully without crashing.

scenario: User can complete a check-in without blocking failure
steps: Answer the questions and submit the check-in.
expected_result: The check-in is completed successfully and the app remains stable.

scenario: User can change language setting
expected_result: The selected language is applied and saved.

scenario: User can enable secure access
steps: Find the security or privacy settings.
expected_result: The app requests the configured secure access method on reopening.

scenario: Privacy explanation appears before consent completion
steps: Start onboarding as a new user.
Proceed to the point where personal data consent is requested.
expected_result: The app displays a readable explanation of collected data and its purpose before consent is finalized.

scenario: User can decline consent and exit onboarding
steps: Start onboarding as a new user.
Choose not to accept consent terms.
expected_result: The user can exit or stop onboarding without being forced deeper into setup.

scenario: User can access privacy-related settings
expected_result: Privacy settings are visible and accessible.

scenario: User can request account data deletion
steps: Select the data deletion or account deletion option.
expected_result: The app provides a visible option to request deletion of stored account data.

scenario: User can access coping guidance from the app
steps: Navigate to support, guidance, or help content.
expected_result: The app provides accessible emotional support or coping guidance.

scenario: Support content is easy to reach during distress
steps: Open the app from the home or check-in screen.
Try to access emotional support content.
expected_result: Support content is reachable within a 2 steps and uses calm, non-judgmental wording.

scenario: High risk response triggers crisis support options
steps: Provide responses indicating self-harm or severe distress.
expected_result: The app immediately displays crisis support options.

scenario: Crisis support includes direct action methods
expected_result: At least one direct support action such as call, text, or external contact is provided.

scenario: Support options include more than one communication channel
expected_result: The app provides more than one support communication option where possible.

scenario: Support content does not assume all users can make voice calls
steps: Open the crisis or support page.
Inspect the available support methods and wording.
expected_result: The app does not rely only on voice-call support and does not assume all users can call.

scenario: Prompts change based on previous responses
steps: Submit several mood check-ins with a consistent pattern.
Continue using the app over later sessions.
expected_result: Later prompts reflect the user's previous responses or recurring concerns.

scenario: Repeated prompts are reduced when responses are similar
steps: Provide similar responses across multiple repetitive check-ins.
Monitor the prompts in future sessions.
expected_result: The app reduces unnecessary repetition and provides more relevant follow-up content.
